commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=31a2a968ae14c485e1caa97f1786804871375136)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds recent download tracking to the Browser Playwright page state and threads a `downloads` payload through `/act` responses for click/evaluate/batch actions.

PR surface: Source +51. Total +51 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. The current and PR code show a click-triggered download is saved asynchronously while `/act` can return before that save is recorded, so the reported missing confirmation can still happen.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR should stay with human/author review because it needs contributor real behavior proof and maintainer choice between this narrow notification shape and the broader download-action API path.

target.title: fix(browser): notify agent when click triggers download
target.body:
## Summary
- Track auto-saved page downloads in `PageState.recentDownloads[]` so the background handler records every completed download
- Capture the download delta in `executeActViaPlaywright` before/after each action
- Propagate `downloads` info through the `POST /act` response to the agent

Fixes #93250

## Linked context
- When a browser `click` action triggers a file download, the auto-save handler in `pw-session.ts` saves it to disk but the agent receives only `{ok: true}` with no download confirmation
- The agent has no way to know the download succeeded, so it keeps retrying different strategies
- This change records downloads in page state and includes them in the action response

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: After a click triggers a download, the agent response now includes download information

**Real setup tested**: Runtime: node on Linux

**Exact steps or command run after fix**:

```bash
cd extensions/browser
npx vitest run --config ../../test/vitest/vitest.extension-browser.config.ts
```

**After-fix evidence**:

```
 Test Files  120 passed | 1 failed (121)
      Tests  1383 passed | 1 failed | 1 skipped (1385)
```

(1 pre-existing failure in `cdp-proxy-bypass.test.ts`, unrelated to this change)

**Observed result after the fix**: All download-related tests pass (10/10), and no existing tests regressed

**What was not tested**: End-to-end browser click-to-download scenario with a real web page (requires live browser automation)

**Proof limitations or environment constraints**: Unit tests verify state tracking and response propagation; full E2E would require a browser with a real download-triggering page

## Tests and validation

- `pw-tools-core.shared.test.ts` — 2 passed
- `pw-tools-core.waits-next-download-saves-it.test.ts` — 10 passed
- Full browser extension test suite — 1383/1384 passed

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes`)
- `POST /act` responses for click/clickCoords/batch actions may now include a `downloads` field

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`No`)

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`No`)

What is the highest-risk area?
- The `downloads` field in responses is additive and only appears when downloads occur; existing clients that do not expect it will ignore it

How is that risk mitigated?
- The `downloads` key is only included when `newDownloads.length > 0`; the `jsonOk` helper spreads it as extra fields, preserving backward compatibility

## Current review state

What is the next action?
- Maintainer review

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?
- CI checks to pass

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?
- N/A (new PR)
